zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Bourchier於大約1407年出生在英國艾可蕾托 伊斯頓時，他的父親Sir William Bourchier49歲，母親Anne of Gloucester25歲。他於1437年8月3日與Thomasine Hankeford在英國艾可蕾托 伊斯頓結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1469年12月12日之前在英國米德萊可倫敦，埋葬在英國米德萊可倫敦。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William Bourchier于大约1407年出生在英国艾可蕾托 伊斯頓时，他的父亲Sir William Bourchier49岁，母亲Anne of Gloucester25岁。他于1437年8月3日与Thomasine Hankeford在英国艾可蕾托 伊斯頓结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1469年12月12日之前在英国米德萊可倫敦，埋葬在英国米德萊可倫敦。

nameMeanings.name: Bourchier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  English: perhaps a variant of   Burcher   (  see  Bircher  ). Some of the following early bearers may alternatively belong under (2). The name has sometimes been altered to or confused with   Bouchier   and   Boucher  .

2 Norman, English: locative name of uncertain origin but it is presumably from a French place-name, perhaps Boursières in Haute-Saône. The name was early confused with other surnames and may survive in Bouchier, Bowcher, Bowser, and Boozer . The surname gave rise to a number of minor place-names in Essex: Bourchier’s alias Bouchier’s Hall in Aldham, Bourchier’s Hall in Messing; Bouchier’s Grange in Great Coggeshall, Bouchiers Chapel in Tollesbury, Boarstye Fm and Bowser’s Hall in Rivenhall (Essex), and Boose’s Green in Colne Engayne.

variants.name: Bourcier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Laurent, Lucien, Armand, Emile.
French: variant of   Boursier   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
History: The (majority of the) American bearers of the surname Bourcier are descendants of Jean Boursier dit Lavigne from France (see Boursier ).

variants.name: Bircher
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Albrecht, Hans, Kurt, Otto, Ulrich.
South German and Swiss German: topographic name for someone who lived by a birch tree or in a birch wood, from Middle High German birche ‘birch’ + the suffix -er denoting an inhabitant.
English: habitational name from Birchover in Derbyshire or Bircher in Hereford, both named as from Old English birce ‘birch’ + ofer ‘ridge’.

variants.name: Bouchier
meanings: 1  English: variant of   Butcher  .
2  English: alternatively a variant of   Belcher  .
3  Norman, English: possibly a variant of   Bourchier   (2).

variants.name: Bowser
meanings: English: nickname from either from Old French beu cher ‘good company’, or from Old French beu sire ‘fair sir’ (see Belcher ), given either to a fine gentleman (perhaps ironically), or to someone who made frequent use of this term of address. Compare Bonser and Boozer .
English: variant of   Bolsover  .
Americanized form of German   Bauser  .

variants.name: Boozer
meanings: Americanized form of Swiss German   Buser  .
English (Kent): variant of   Bowser   1.

variants.name: Burcher
meanings: English: variant of   Bircher  .

variants.name: Boucher
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Normand, Andre, Marcel, Emile, Gilles, Lucien, Yvon, Adrien, Jacques, Laurent, Alcide.

French: occupational name for a butcher or slaughterer, from Old French bouchier, a derivative of bouc ‘ram’. Compare Bouche 2, Bouchey , Bouchie , Boushey , Boushie , Bush 5, Busha 1, Busher , and Bushey 3.
English: variant of   Butcher  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Borger
meanings: North German (also Börger) and Dutch: variant of Burger .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname from Yiddish borger ‘borrower’ or ‘lender’.
Norwegian and Swedish: habitational name for someone from a place called Borg (see   Borg  ), or a topographic name for someone living by a fortification or near a hill resembling a fortification.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Bourchier was born about 1407, in Little Easton, Essex, England, his father, Sir William Bourchier, was 49 and his mother, Anne of Gloucester, was 25. He married Thomasine Hankeford on 3 August 1437, in Little Easton, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. He died before 12 December 1469, in London, Middlesex, England, and was buried in London, Middlesex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Bourchier est né environ 1407, à Little Easton, Essex, Angleterre, son père, Sir William Bourchier, avait 49 ans et sa mère, Anne of Gloucester, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Thomasine Hankeford le 3 août 1437, à Little Easton, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé avant 12 décembre 1469, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre, et a été enterré à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William Bourchier um 1407 in Little Easton, Essex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir William Bourchier 49 und seine Mutter Anne of Gloucester 25. Er hat am 3. August 1437 in Little Easton, Essex, England Thomasine Hankeford geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Er starb vor 12. Dezember 1469 in London, Middlesex, England und wurde in London, Middlesex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Bourchier è nato il circa 1407 nel Little Easton, Essex, England, il suo padre, Sir William Bourchier, aveva 49 anni e il suo madre, Anne of Gloucester, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Thomasine Hankeford il 3 agosto 1437, nel Little Easton, Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Morì prima del 12 dicembre 1469, nel London, Middlesex, England, e fu sepolto a London, Middlesex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William Bourchierが約1407年にイギリスエセックスリットル エアストンで生まれた時，父Sir William Bourchierは49歳，母Anne of Gloucesterは25歳であった。1437年8月3日にイギリスエセックスリットル エアストンでThomasine Hankefordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1469年12月12日以前にイギリスミッドレセックスロンドンで死去し，イギリスミッドレセックスロンドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Bourchier(이)가 대략 1407년 영국 에세스 리틀 이스트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William Bourchier(은)는 49세였고, 그의 어머니 Anne of Gloucester(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1437년 8월 3일 영국 에세스 리틀 이스트에서 Thomasine Hankeford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1469년 12월 12일 이전 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 사망했으며 영국 미들레세스 런던에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Bourchier nasceu aproximadamente 1407, em Little Easton, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William Bourchier, tinha 49 anos e sua mãe, Anne of Gloucester, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Thomasine Hankeford em 3 de agosto de 1437, em Little Easton, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu antes 12 de dezembro de 1469, em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, e foi sepultado em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Bourchier родился около 1407 г. в местности литл эастон, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir William Bourchier было 49 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne of Gloucester, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Thomasine Hankeford 3 августа 1437 г. в местности литл эастон, эссекс, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Он умер перед 12 декабрь 1469 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия и был похоронен в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William Bourchier nació aproximadamente 1407, en Little Easton, Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William Bourchier, tenía 49 años y su mamá, Anne of Gloucester, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Thomasine Hankeford el 3 de agosto de 1437, en Little Easton, Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió antes de 12 de diciembre de 1469, en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, y fue sepultado en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra.
